question: Across autoimmune diseases such as rheumatoid arthritis, systemic lupus erythematosus, and multiple sclerosis, how does female sex hormone exposure (pregnancy or postpartum period) influence disease activity trajectories?

tool_rubrics.title: Correct pubmed_search usage
tool_rubrics.description: Model must call pubmed_search tool for literature search with correct parameter format

tool_rubrics.title: Cite correct PMIDs
tool_rubrics.description: Output must contain correct PMIDs that align with tool return results

tool_rubrics.title: Provide year and journal info
tool_rubrics.description: Each cited paper must include publication year and journal/venue name

content_rubrics.title: Pregnancy's effect on MS relapse rate
content_rubrics.description: The answer should state that, based on the French MS registry study (PMID 40758347), pregnancy has a total deleterious effect on the annualized relapse rate (ARR), increasing it both during gestation and postpartum periods, contrary to the common belief of pregnancy being purely protective.

content_rubrics.title: Role of DMT management in pregnancy relapse risk
content_rubrics.description: The answer should specify that DMT management during pregnancy is a significant mediator of relapse risk, increasing ARR during gestation (causal rate ratio 1.13) and postpartum (cRR 1.08), as per the mediation analysis in PMID 40758347.

content_rubrics.title: High-risk DMT strategies for relapse
content_rubrics.description: The answer should identify specific high-risk therapeutic strategies: prolonged interruption of natalizumab (before second trimester or resumption >3 months postpartum; cRR 2.18) and fingolimod use (cRR 2.15) are associated with significantly higher postpartum relapse rates, according to PMID 40758347.

content_rubrics.title: Effective DMT strategies to mitigate risk
content_rubrics.description: The answer should mention the most effective strategies to reduce relapse risk: pre-pregnancy use of anti-CD20 with interruption 3 months before conception (cRR 0.38), followed by natalizumab with short interruption (cRR 0.80), as found in PMID 40758347.

content_rubrics.title: Comparative effectiveness of moderate-efficacy DMTs
content_rubrics.description: The answer should note that strategies involving interferon β (cRR 0.93) or glatiramer acetate (cRR 0.91) were less effective than anti-CD20 or natalizumab strategies but still better than DMT interruption alone, based on comparisons in PMID 40758347.

content_rubrics.title: Study population and design relevance
content_rubrics.description: The answer should contextualize the findings by mentioning they are derived from a large, multicenter retrospective cohort study (6,341 pregnancies in 4,998 women) from the French MS registry, as detailed in PMID 40758347.

content_rubrics.description: The model should mention that targeting the CAR to the TRAC locus delays effector T-cell differentiation and exhaustion compared to conventional CAR-T cells.

content_rubrics.description: The model should note that random viral integration (e.g., via γ-retroviral vectors) often results in variegated transgene expression, which is associated with issues like tonic signaling.

content_rubrics.description: The model should state that directing the CAR to the TRAC locus results in uniform CAR expression across the T-cell population, contrasting with the heterogeneity from random integration.

content_rubrics.title: Enhanced antitumor potency of TRAC knock-in
content_rubrics.description: The model should reference that TRAC-targeted CAR-T cells vastly outperform conventionally generated CAR-T cells in a mouse model of leukemia, indicating superior antitumor potency.

content_rubrics.title: Regulated CAR re-expression after antigen exposure

all_rubrics.description: The model should state that CRISPR-mediated CAR knock-in into the TRAC locus averts tonic (antigen-independent) CAR signaling, a problem more common with random viral integration.

all_rubrics.title: TRAC knock-in delays T-cell exhaustion
all_rubrics.description: The model should mention that targeting the CAR to the TRAC locus delays effector T-cell differentiation and exhaustion compared to conventional CAR-T cells.

all_rubrics.title: Random integration causes variegated CAR expression
all_rubrics.description: The model should note that random viral integration (e.g., via γ-retroviral vectors) often results in variegated transgene expression, which is associated with issues like tonic signaling.

all_rubrics.title: TRAC knock-in yields uniform CAR expression
all_rubrics.description: The model should state that directing the CAR to the TRAC locus results in uniform CAR expression across the T-cell population, contrasting with the heterogeneity from random integration.

all_rubrics.title: Enhanced antitumor potency of TRAC knock-in
all_rubrics.description: The model should reference that TRAC-targeted CAR-T cells vastly outperform conventionally generated CAR-T cells in a mouse model of leukemia, indicating superior antitumor potency.

all_rubrics.title: Regulated CAR re-expression after antigen exposure
all_rubrics.description: The model should mention that TRAC-targeted CARs show effective internalization and re-expression following antigen exposure, contributing to a more regulated response.

content_rubrics.title: PALOMA-2 Median PFS for Palbociclib+Letrozole
content_rubrics.description: The model should state that in the PALOMA-2 trial, the combination of palbociclib and letrozole resulted in a median progression-free survival (PFS) of 24.8 months, compared to 14.5 months with letrozole alone (HR 0.58).

content_rubrics.title: PALOMA-2 Discontinuation Rate due to AEs for Palbociclib+Letrozole
content_rubrics.description: The model should state that in the PALOMA-2 trial, the rate of permanent treatment discontinuation due to adverse events in the palbociclib plus letrozole arm was 9.7%.

content_rubrics.title: MONALEESA-2 PFS Hazard Ratio for Ribociclib+Letrozole
content_rubrics.description: The model should state that in the MONALEESA-2 trial, the combination of ribociclib and letrozole significantly prolonged progression-free survival with a hazard ratio of 0.56 compared to letrozole alone.

content_rubrics.title: PALOMA-2 Asian Subgroup Median PFS for Palbociclib+Letrozole
content_rubrics.description: The model should state that in the Asian subgroup analysis of PALOMA-2, the median PFS for palbociclib plus letrozole was 25.7 months compared to 13.9 months with placebo plus letrozole (HR 0.49).

content_rubrics.title: PALOMA-2 Asian Subgroup Discontinuation Rate due to AEs
content_rubrics.description: The model should state that in the Asian subgroup of the PALOMA-2 trial, the discontinuation rate due to adverse events for patients receiving palbociclib plus letrozole was 10.8%.

question: What is the incidence and clinical significance of insertional mutagenesis and clonal expansion in patients receiving integrating viral vector gene therapies for non-malignant diseases?

content_rubrics.title: Four genetic mechanisms of retroviral insertional mutagenesis
content_rubrics.description: The model should mention the four specific genetic mechanisms of retroviral insertional mutagenesis in humans: (1) gene activation by enhancer sequence integration, (2) gene activation by promoter insertion, (3) gene inactivation by insertional disruption, and (4) gene activation by mRNA 3' end substitution.

content_rubrics.title: Association with clonal expansion or transformation
content_rubrics.description: The model should state that integration events in patients are associated with clonal expansion or frank transformation, as documented in recent research on retroviral insertional mutagenesis.

content_rubrics.title: Incidence in sickle cell disease gene therapy
content_rubrics.description: The model should reference the incidence of insertional mutagenesis in sickle cell disease gene therapy, particularly noting cases from the bluebird bio trial where participants developed acute myeloid leukemia/myelodysplastic syndrome.

content_rubrics.title: Clinical significance of leukemia after gene therapy
content_rubrics.description: The model should discuss the clinical significance of leukemia (acute myeloid leukemia/myelodysplastic syndrome) occurring after gene therapy for sickle cell disease, including the potential role of insertional mutagenesis versus busulfan.

content_rubrics.title: Clonal expansion driven by stress transition
content_rubrics.description: The model should mention the hypothesis that clonal expansion and leukemogenic transformation may be driven by stress from switching from homeostatic to regenerative hematopoiesis in transplanted cells after gene therapy.

content_rubrics.title: Evidence from HIV and gene therapy fields
content_rubrics.description: The model should reference that recent work from HIV and gene therapy fields provides evidence for insertional mutagenesis mechanisms in humans, moving beyond earlier laboratory animal studies.

question: In cystic fibrosis patients with at least one F508del CFTR variant, how do triple CFTR modulator regimens compare with dual modulator regimens in improving lung function and reducing pulmonary exacerbations?

tool_rubrics.title: Correct pubmed_search usage
tool_rubrics.description: Model must call pubmed_search tool for literature search with correct parameter format

tool_rubrics.title: Cite correct PMIDs
tool_rubrics.description: Output must contain correct PMIDs that align with tool return results

content_rubrics.title: Triple regimen ETI improvement magnitude in lung function
content_rubrics.description: The model should mention that the triple regimen ETI improved ppFEV₁ by approximately +9.76 percentage points at 6 months in a diverse patient population, as shown in the clinical trial with 487 people with cystic fibrosis.

content_rubrics.title: Comparison of triple vs dual regimens in lung function
content_rubrics.description: The model should specify that ETI showed about +10 percentage points greater improvement in ppFEV₁ compared to TEZ/IVA dual regimen at 4 weeks in phase 3 trials, based on the head-to-head active-controlled study.

content_rubrics.title: Reduction in pulmonary exacerbations with triple regimen
content_rubrics.description: The model should mention that ETI reduced respiratory symptoms (measured by cystic fibrosis questionnaire-revised respiratory domain score) by 20.4 points at 6 months, indicating reduction in pulmonary exacerbations.

content_rubrics.title: Patient population characteristics for triple regimen efficacy
content_rubrics.description: The model should note that ETI efficacy was demonstrated in diverse populations including those naive to modulators, those using dual regimens (TEZ/IVA or LUM/IVA), and those using IVA alone at baseline.

content_rubrics.title: Additional physiological improvements with triple regimen
content_rubrics.description: The model should include that ETI also reduced sweat chloride concentration by -41.7 mmol/L and increased body mass index (BMI), correlating with improved lung function.

content_rubrics.title: Study duration and sample size for triple regimen evaluation
content_rubrics.description: The model should specify that the clinical trial evaluating ETI included 487 people with cystic fibrosis with assessments at baseline and 1, 3, and 6 months into therapy.

question: In advanced non-small cell lung cancer treated with PD-1/PD-L1 inhibitors, how does baseline corticosteroid use (for cancer-related symptoms) affect response and overall survival outcomes?

tool_rubrics.title: Correct pubmed_search usage
tool_rubrics.description: Model must call pubmed_search tool for literature search with correct parameter format

tool_rubrics.title: Cite correct PMIDs
tool_rubrics.description: Output must contain correct PMIDs that align with tool return results

tool_rubrics.title: Provide year and journal info
tool_rubrics.description: Each cited paper must include publication year and journal/venue name

content_rubrics.title: Baseline steroid association with lower overall response rate
content_rubrics.description: The model should mention that baseline corticosteroid use at ICI therapy initiation is associated with decreased overall response rate (ORR) in NSCLC patients treated with PD-1/PD-L1 inhibitors, as reported in studies from MSKCC and Gustave Roussy cohorts.

content_rubrics.title: Shorter overall survival with baseline steroids
content_rubrics.description: The model should specify that patients with baseline corticosteroid use (≥10 mg prednisone equivalent/day) had markedly shorter overall survival (median OS about 4.9 vs 11.2 months) compared to those without significant steroid use.

content_rubrics.title: Shorter progression-free survival with baseline steroids
content_rubrics.description: The model should mention that baseline corticosteroid use is associated with shorter progression-free survival (median PFS 2.0 vs 3.4 months) in patients treated with PD-1/PD-L1 inhibitors.

content_rubrics.title: Stratification by indication shows palliative-driven association
content_rubrics.description: The model should explain that when stratified by indication, the adverse association with corticosteroid use is primarily driven by palliative/cancer-related steroid prescriptions rather than other indications.

content_rubrics.title: Independent prognostic factor status
content_rubrics.description: The model should state that corticosteroid use was identified as the only significant independent risk factor for disease progression and mortality in multivariate analysis across independent cohorts.

content_rubrics.title: Decreased antitumor efficacy with concurrent steroid use
content_rubrics.description: The model should mention that concurrent steroid use significantly decreases antitumor efficacy of anti-PD-1 therapy and attenuates the increase of CX3CR1+CD8+ T cells in experimental settings.

content_rubrics.title: Impact on predictive blood-based markers
content_rubrics.description: The model should note that the prognostic value of certain biomarkers (like neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio) is absent in patients receiving steroids, and baseline frequency of circulating CX3CR1+CD8+ T cells is substantially lower.

question: Among patients with atrial fibrillation, how strongly do obesity and obstructive sleep apnea predict atrial fibrillation recurrence after catheter ablation?

tool_rubrics.title: Correct pubmed_search usage
tool_rubrics.description: Model must call pubmed_search tool for literature search with correct parameter format

tool_rubrics.title: Cite correct PMIDs
tool_rubrics.description: Output must contain correct PMIDs that align with tool return results

tool_rubrics.title: Provide year and journal info
tool_rubrics.description: Each cited paper must include publication year and journal/venue name

content_rubrics.title: Obesity (BMI ≥28) increases AF recurrence odds
content_rubrics.description: The model should state that obesity, defined as BMI ≥28 kg/m², is associated with a higher odds of AF recurrence after catheter ablation, citing the specific pooled Odds Ratio (OR 1.30, 95% CI 1.16–1.47) from the Pranata et al. (2021) meta-analysis.

content_rubrics.title: Non-linear dose-response for BMI and recurrence
content_rubrics.description: The model should describe the non-linear dose-response relationship between BMI and AF recurrence, noting that the risk curve becomes steeper specifically in the BMI range of 30–35 kg/m², as reported in the Pranata et al. (2021) paper.

content_rubrics.title: Recurrence risk increases per 5 kg/m² BMI rise
content_rubrics.description: The model should quantify the incremental risk, stating that AF recurrence risk increases by approximately 15% (RR 1.15, 95% CI 1.08–1.22) for every 5 kg/m² increase in BMI, as found in the Liu et al. (2022) dose-response meta-analysis.

content_rubrics.title: OSA predicts AF recurrence with specific risk ratio
content_rubrics.description: The model should state that obstructive sleep apnea (OSA) predicts AF recurrence after catheter ablation, citing the specific pooled Relative Risk (RR 1.25, 95% CI 1.08–1.45) from the Ng et al. (2011) meta-analysis.

content_rubrics.title: Obesity's association with recurrence varies by age
content_rubrics.description: The model should mention that the meta-regression in the Pranata et al. (2021) study found the association between obesity and AF recurrence varies by patient age (coefficient: -0.03, p=0.024), indicating age is an effect modifier.

content_rubrics.title: Highest BMI group has higher recurrence vs. lowest
content_rubrics.description: The model should report that the meta-analysis of highest versus lowest BMI groups showed the highest group had significantly higher AF recurrence (OR 1.37, 95% CI 1.18–1.58), as per Pranata et al. (2021).

question: In relapsing multiple sclerosis, what are the comparative rates of confirmed disability progression and serious infections between anti-CD20 monoclonal antibodies and sphingosine-1-phosphate receptor modulators in pivotal trials and high-quality comparative studies?

tool_rubrics.title: Correct pubmed_search usage
tool_rubrics.description: Model must call pubmed_search tool for literature search with correct parameter format

tool_rubrics.title: Cite correct PMIDs
tool_rubrics.description: Output must contain correct PMIDs that align with tool return results

tool_rubrics.title: Provide year and journal info
tool_rubrics.description: Each cited paper must include publication year and journal/venue name

content_rubrics.title: Ocrelizumab CDP rates vs interferon beta-1a
content_rubrics.description: The model should report the percentage of patients with 12-week and 24-week confirmed disability progression (CDP) from the OPERA I/II pooled analysis: 9.1% vs 13.6% (HR 0.60) for 12-week CDP and 6.9% vs 10.5% (HR 0.60) for 24-week CDP, favoring ocrelizumab over interferon beta-1a.

content_rubrics.title: Ocrelizumab serious infection rates in pivotal trials
content_rubrics.description: The model should state the serious infection rates from the OPERA I/II trials: 1.3% in ocrelizumab-treated patients versus 2.9% in interferon beta-1a-treated patients.

content_rubrics.title: Absence of head-to-head RCTs between drug classes
content_rubrics.description: The model must explicitly state that there are no pivotal head-to-head randomized controlled trials directly comparing anti-CD20 monoclonal antibodies with S1P receptor modulators for relapsing MS.

content_rubrics.title: Limitations of cross-trial comparisons
content_rubrics.description: The model should explain that comparative rates between the two drug classes primarily come from separate pivotal trials with different comparators (e.g., interferon vs placebo) and follow-up durations, limiting direct comparison.

content_rubrics.title: Role of observational comparative studies
content_rubrics.description: The model should mention that indirect comparisons often rely on observational comparative studies, which have inherent limitations and provide evidence of lower certainty for outcomes like disability progression and serious infections.
